title: aichryson tortuosum subsp. bethencourtianum
intro: Aichryson tortuosum subsp. bethencourtianum, also known as Bethencourt's aloe, is a succulent plant species native to the Canary Islands. It belongs to the Crassulaceae family and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions, including wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Aichryson tortuosum subsp. bethencourtianum can be used in herbal preparations such as ointments, creams, and tinctures, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
